action.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3629

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 5, "B93": 793, "A11": 21, "B33": 842, "C08": 313, "B39": 842, "B96": 631, "C85": 226, "A82": 87, "B31": 761, "B20": 230, "A60": 287, "B09": 698, "C81": 641, "B21": 882}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 868, "B33": 822, "C08": 783, "B39": 65, "B96": 262, "C85": 121, "A82": 508, "B31": 780, "B20": 461, "A60": 484, "B09": 668, "C81": 389, "B21": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
action.label: context

action.content:
I've analyzed the items based on their effort and my hidden importance scores. My goal is to secure the set of items with the highest total importance for me. I will start by making a strong, near-optimal proposal. This anchors the negotiation in my favor. I will calculate the total effort of my chosen items to ensure it's under the limit of 3629.

My chosen set is: {'A11', 'A21', 'A82', 'C08', 'B20', 'A60', 'B31', 'B33', 'B09', 'C85'}.
Effort calculation:
- A11: 21
- A21: 5
- A82: 87
- C08: 313
- B20: 230
- A60: 287
- B31: 761
- B33: 842
- B09: 698
- C85: 226
Total effort = 21 + 5 + 87 + 313 + 230 + 287 + 761 + 842 + 698 + 226 = 3470.
3470 is ≤ 3629, so this is a valid proposal.

This set is very good for me, as it prioritizes items with high importance-to-effort ratios and excludes items that are inefficient for me (like B39, which has a very low importance for a very high effort).

I will now formulate my first message. It must contain an `ARGUMENT` and can contain a `PROPOSAL`. I will include both to be assertive. My argument will be a general statement about the quality of the proposed set without revealing my secret scores.

PROPOSAL: {'A11', 'A21', 'A82', 'C08', 'B20', 'A60', 'B31', 'B33', 'B09', 'C85'}
I've chosen this set. Let's check the rules again. "A tag ... must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings." "Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores."
My argument will be a vague justification.

ARGUMENT: {'This collection represents a strong and efficient use of our shared effort limit.'}
This should be a valid opening move. I'm proposing a very favorable deal for myself and using a mild argument to support it.
I need to make sure the output is formatted correctly.
The structure is fine. It's time to generate the response.
I am ready.
The user is the other participant. I am the AI. I will generate the first message.
The message should not have any extra text outside the structured formats.
The message has to be non-empty. My message will contain two structured formats, so it is non-empty.
I've re-checked all my logic and the rules. I'm confident in this first move.
Let's do it.PROPOSAL: {'A11', 'A21', 'A82', 'C08', 'B20', 'A60', 'B31', 'B33', 'B09', 'C85'}
ARGUMENT: {'This proposal offers a comprehensive and robust set of items, making very efficient use of our shared effort budget.'}
action.label: response

action.content: Your response contained an untagged sequence or you used STRATEGIC REASONING more than once. You may only use the structured formats as explained in the initial message. They must all be of the form TAG: {...}
